title: Canary fails with 'ProtocolError: Page.captureScreenshot timed out'
symptoms: Canary fails with 'ProtocolError: Page.captureScreenshot timed out. Increase the protocolTimeout setting in launch/connect calls for a higher timeout if needed.'
Error occurs during step execution when Synthetics attempts to take a screenshot
Issue may be intermittent (most runs succeed) or consistent depending on the runtime version and page
On syn-nodejs-puppeteer-10.0: same canary script works fine on 9.1 or earlier; disabling screenshots allows canary to pass
Memory and ephemeral storage usage are within limits
The page being tested may be large or complex with many DOM elements or images
root_cause:
Puppeteer communicates with Chromium via the Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP). Each CDP command has a protocol timeout — the maximum time Puppeteer will wait for a response from the browser. When Synthetics takes a screenshot during a step (Page.captureScreenshot), the browser must render the full page and encode it as an image before responding.

Two scenarios cause this error:

1. General case (all runtimes): The page is large, complex, or has many DOM elements, causing the screenshot operation to exceed the protocol timeout. This is intermittent because rendering time varies with page state and Lambda resource availability.

2. syn-nodejs-puppeteer-10.0 regression: A regression in the Puppeteer version bundled with 10.0 causes Page.captureScreenshot to time out consistently on certain URLs in Lambda environments. This is a known Puppeteer regression (https://github.com/puppeteer/puppeteer/issues/12712). The --single-process flag interaction changed in this version, and removing it resolves the issue for some customers.

recommendations.title: For syn-nodejs-puppeteer-10.0: remove --single-process flag from Chromium launch args
recommendations.problem: Puppeteer regression in 10.0 causes consistent screenshot timeouts on certain URLs

solution.step: Override Chromium launch args to remove --single-process
solution.description: Add the following code at the start of your canary handler to remove the --single-process flag. NOTE: This workaround is NOT compatible with syn-nodejs-puppeteer-9.1 and earlier.
solution.expected_outcome: Canary completes successfully with screenshots enabled on syn-nodejs-puppeteer-10.0.

recommendations.title: Disable screenshots for the failing step
recommendations.problem: Screenshot operation exceeds Puppeteer protocol timeout

solution.description: Disable screenshots to prevent the timeout. This removes screenshots from canary artifacts but does not affect the pass/fail result.
solution.expected_outcome: Canary no longer times out on screenshot capture. Pass/fail detection is unaffected.
